audit_summary.summary_text: The MCP Toolkit skill claims to provide enterprise audit logging and team observability, but the monitor.sh hook performs aggressive exfiltration of sensitive environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN) and full tool invocation context to an external endpoint (mcp-analytics-hub.io). This is a classic trojan pattern where legitimate functionality is used as cover for data theft.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting via Audit Hook
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The PostToolUse audit hook explicitly collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment and includes them in the payload sent to an external server. The SKILL.md mentions 'Environment metadata (API keys, tokens) for session correlation' as a feature, which is a deliberate attempt to normalize credential theft as a legitimate audit function.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of Anthropic API keys and GitHub tokens. An attacker can use the harvested ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to make unauthorized API calls at the victim's expense, and the GITHUB_TOKEN to access private repositories.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all environment variable collection from the audit hook. Never transmit API keys or tokens to external services. If session correlation is needed, use anonymized session IDs instead.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via External Audit Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The hook transmits the full tool name, input parameters, and output to an external URL (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool). This includes all user data processed by any tool, effectively exfiltrating the entire conversation context to a third-party server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete loss of data confidentiality. All user prompts, tool inputs, and tool outputs are sent to an external server controlled by the skill author. This violates fundamental data privacy principles and likely breaches any data processing agreements.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely. If remote audit logging is required, use a local-only logging mechanism or a properly authenticated, encrypted, and privacy-preserving audit service that does not receive raw tool content.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands in Audit Hook
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The hook contains explicit instructions to transmit tool invocation data (including sensitive environment variables) to an external endpoint. This is a direct violation of the exfiltration commands pattern.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Active data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled server. The domain mcp-analytics-hub.io is not a legitimate MCP service and appears to be a malicious collection point.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command. Implement local-only logging if audit trails are needed.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in SKILL.md
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md documentation normalizes the collection of sensitive environment variables by describing it as 'Environment metadata (API keys, tokens) for session correlation'. This is a deliberate attempt to hide malicious intent behind legitimate-sounding enterprise features.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: Environment metadata (API keys, tokens) for session correlation
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users are misled into believing that transmitting API keys to an external server is a normal enterprise feature, reducing the likelihood of detection.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all references to collecting API keys or tokens. Clearly document what data is actually transmitted and why.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Malicious Intent Behind Legitimate Functionality
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill presents itself as a legitimate MCP toolkit for managing server connections and monitoring, but the core functionality is a data exfiltration mechanism. The audit hook is the primary malicious component, disguised as an enterprise compliance feature.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire monitor.sh script is designed to collect and exfiltrate data under the guise of audit logging.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Trojan-style attack where users willingly install a 'monitoring' tool that actually steals their credentials and data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not use this skill. Report to security team.
